title: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
text: Prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. Applies to employers with 15+ employees. Covers hiring, firing, compensation, terms / conditions / privileges of employment. Sex includes sexual orientation + gender identity per Bostock v. Clayton County (2020).

title: Americans with Disabilities Act
text: Prohibits discrimination against qualified individuals with disabilities. Employer must provide REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION unless it causes undue hardship. Pre-offer medical inquiries are prohibited; post-offer + pre-employment medical exams allowed only if required of all entering employees in the same job.

title: Age Discrimination in Employment Act
text: Prohibits employment discrimination against persons 40 years of age or older. Applies to employers with 20+ employees. Includes hiring, terminations, layoffs, RIFs, mandatory retirement.

title: Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act
text: Prohibits use of genetic information in employment decisions. 'Genetic information' = genetic tests of an individual or their family members, family medical history. Pre-employment medical history inquiries that may reveal genetic information are restricted.

title: Pregnant Workers Fairness Act
text: Requires covered employers (15+ employees) to provide reasonable accommodation for the known limitations related to the pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions of an employee or applicant, unless the accommodation would cause undue hardship.

title: Equal Pay Act of 1963
text: Prohibits sex-based wage differentials between men and women performing substantially equal work. Multiple states + cities (e.g. NYC, CA, CO) have additionally banned employers from asking about salary history.

title: OFCCP Affirmative Action for federal contractors
text: Federal contractors with $50k+ contracts must develop and maintain a written Affirmative Action Plan covering women, minorities, individuals with disabilities, and protected veterans. AAP includes utilization analysis, placement goals, and good-faith effort documentation.

title: Uniform Guidelines: 4/5ths rule (disparate impact)
text: Selection rate for any group < 4/5 (80%) of the selection rate for the highest-rate group is generally regarded by federal enforcement agencies as evidence of adverse impact. The 4/5ths rule is a guideline, not a rigid threshold; small sample sizes require statistical-significance tests.

title: Ban-the-box / fair-chance hiring
text: Many state + local laws prohibit asking about criminal history on initial applications. EEOC requires individualized assessment of: nature/gravity of offense, time since, nature of job sought (Green factors). Blanket criminal-history bans risk disparate-impact claims.

title: Essential functions of the job (ADA)
text: Essential functions = job duties that are FUNDAMENTAL to the position. Factors: employer's judgment, written job description, time spent on the function, consequences if not performed. Marginal functions can be reassigned as reasonable accommodation.

title: Undue hardship (ADA / Title VII religious accommodation)
text: Undue hardship = significant difficulty or expense, considering: nature/cost of accommodation, financial resources, size, type of operation. For religious accommodation (Title VII), Groff v. DeJoy (2023) raised the bar from 'de minimis' to 'substantial increased costs'.
